reasoning: The FedRAMP CM-2 control focuses on maintaining a secure baseline configuration, which can be mapped to several Kubernetes security practices. Ensuring least privilege through RBAC aligns with maintaining secure access configurations. NetworkPolicies with default deny rules help establish a secure network baseline. Enabling etcd encryption ensures data at rest is protected, contributing to a secure configuration baseline. Audit logging ensures that changes to configurations are tracked, aligning with maintaining a baseline configuration.
title: Baseline Configuration
